title: Junior Overman (Coalfields CBT) — Mock 01
pattern: Coalfield Sirdar/Overman CBT (100 Q · 100 marks · 120 min · no −ve)

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 3:00 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each hour mark $=30°$.

**Working.** At 3:00 the hands are 3 hour-marks apart $=3\times30°=90°$.

**Answer.** 90°.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: What is 12.5% of 640?

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: A vehicle travels at 45 km/h for 3 hours. Distance covered is _____.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Simple interest on ₹4000 at 10% per annum for 1.5 years is _____.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: A vehicle travels at 72 km/h for 1.5 hours. Distance covered is _____.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of 12, 18, 24, 30, 36 is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum ÷ count.

**Working.** Sum $=120$, count $=5$, so average $=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the word most similar in meaning to 'CURTAIL':
questions.solution: **Answer.** 'Curtail' means to cut short or reduce.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Coal India Limited (CIL) is headquartered in:
questions.solution: **Answer.** CIL's registered office is in Kolkata, West Bengal.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Simple interest on ₹10000 at 6% per annum for 3 years is _____.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of 10, 14, 18, 22, 26 is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum ÷ count.

**Working.** Sum $=90$, count $=5$, so average $=18$.

**Answer.** $18$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: DGMS (Directorate General of Mines Safety) functions under which Union Ministry?
questions.solution: **Answer.** DGMS is under the Ministry of Labour and Employment, Government of India.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: What is 15% of 200?

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the word OPPOSITE in meaning to 'ASCEND':
questions.solution: **Answer.** The opposite of 'ascend' (go up) is 'descend' (go down).

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: The capital of Chhattisgarh is:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Raipur is the capital of Chhattisgarh.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: A vehicle travels at 50 km/h for 4 hours. Distance covered is _____.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: What is 25% of 80?

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the odd one out: Coal, Lignite, Anthracite, Bauxite
questions.solution: **Answer.** Bauxite is an aluminium ore; the rest are ranks/types of coal.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of 8, 16, 24, 32, 40 is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum ÷ count.

**Working.** Sum $=120$, count $=5$, so average $=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: A vehicle travels at 60 km/h for 2.5 hours. Distance covered is _____.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: A sum of ₹300 is divided between two persons in the ratio 4:1. The larger share is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Share $=\dfrac{\text{part}}{\text{sum of parts}}\times\text{total}$.

**Working.** Parts $=4+1=5$; larger share $=\dfrac{4}{5}\times 300=240$.

**Answer.** ₹$240$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 18 m² at a mean velocity of 3.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Before firing a shot in a gassy coal seam, the shot-firer must test for inflammable gas using a:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Gas testing before shotfiring uses a flame safety lamp or approved methanometer.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: In metalliferous mines, the statutory first-line supervisor equivalent to the coal-mine Sirdar is the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Mining Mate is the first-line supervisor under the MMR 1961.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A deposit holds 30 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 2 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{30}{2}=15$ years.

**Answer.** $15$ years.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: 'Firedamp' in coal mines refers mainly to:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Firedamp is predominantly methane (CH₄), explosive in the 5–15% range.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 8 m² at a mean velocity of 1.8 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine pump delivers 0.06 m³/s of water against a head of 100 m at 72% efficiency. The input power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=\dfrac{\rho g Q H}{\eta}$ with $\rho=1000$ kg/m³, $g=9.81$ m/s².

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{1000\times9.81\times0.06\times100}{0.72}\div1000=81.75$ kW.

**Answer.** $81.75$ kW.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.1 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 15 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A main fan develops a pressure of 2200 Pa while passing 35 m³/s of air. The air power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=\dfrac{P\times Q}{1000}$ kW ($P$ in Pa, $Q$ in m³/s).

**Working.** $\dfrac{2200\times35}{1000}=77$ kW.

**Answer.** $77$ kW.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 2 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 20 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: In bord-and-pillar mining, the operation of extracting the coal pillars is called:
questions.solution: **Answer.** First the area is developed into pillars (bords); later the pillars are extracted — depillaring.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Two ore blocks assay: 200 t at 1.5% and 300 t at 2.5%. The combined (tonnage-weighted) grade is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 20 m² at a mean velocity of 1.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 20 m² at a mean velocity of 2.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A DC mine pump motor draws 1100 W from a 110 V supply. The line current is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A dump truck of 45 t payload completes a 21-minute haul cycle over a 420-minute working shift. Its production is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trips $=\dfrac{\text{shift}}{\text{cycle}}$; output $=$ trips $\times$ payload.

**Working.** Trips $=\dfrac{420}{21}=20$; output $=20\times45=900$ t.

**Answer.** $900$ t.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: In bord-and-pillar development with square pillars of side 20 m and galleries 4 m wide, the percentage of coal extracted on development is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For square pillars (centre spacing $a+w$), development extraction $=\left(1-\dfrac{a^2}{(a+w)^2}\right)\times100$.

**Working.** $\left(1-\dfrac{20^2}{24^2}\right)\times100=30.56\%$.

**Answer.** $30.56\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A blast detonates 36 kg of explosive per delay and a structure stands 150 m away. The scaled distance $\left(D/\sqrt{W}\right)$ is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scaled distance $SD=\dfrac{D}{\sqrt{W}}$ governs peak particle velocity in blast-vibration control.

**Working.** $\dfrac{150}{\sqrt{36}}=\dfrac{150}{6}=25$.

**Answer.** $25$ m/kg$^{1/2}$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.6 m³/min into a roadway carrying 40 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A shovel with a 4 m³ bucket works at a fill factor of 0.8 and a cycle time of 20 s. Its theoretical output is _____.

questions.stem: An ore block of 1500 m³ has bulk density 3.2 t/m³. Its tonnage is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A shovel with a 7 m³ bucket works at a fill factor of 0.85 and a cycle time of 30 s. Its theoretical output is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Under the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017, gassy seams are classified into how many degrees?
questions.solution: **Answer.** CMR 2017 classifies gassy seams as first, second and third degree.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 18 m² at a mean velocity of 2.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Settling (sedimentation) ponds at a mine are provided to:
options: Remove suspended solids before water is discharged
questions.solution: **Answer.** Settling ponds let suspended solids settle so discharged water meets effluent norms.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.5 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 20 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.

questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 12 m² at a mean velocity of 2 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.2 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 10 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: In longwall mining the immediate roof behind the supports is allowed to:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Longwall caving lets the goaf roof collapse behind the powered supports as the face advances.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A deposit holds 12 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 1.5 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{12}{1.5}=8$ years.

**Answer.** $8$ years.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A loaded train of mass 7500 kg is hauled up a gradient of 1 in 20. With a tractive (rolling) resistance coefficient of 0.025, the rope pull is approximately _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: An opencast blast block has burden 7 m, spacing 5 m, bench height 11 m and rock density 2.6 t/m³. If 192.5 kg of explosive is used, the powder factor (tonnes broken per kg) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** PF $=\dfrac{\text{rock broken (t)}}{\text{explosive (kg)}}$.

**Working.** Volume $=7\times5\times11=385$ m³; mass $=385\times2.6=1001$ t; PF $=\dfrac{1001}{192.5}=5.2$ t/kg.

**Answer.** $5.2$ t/kg.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A key thrust of the National Mineral Policy, 2019 is to:
options: Encourage exploration and reduce import dependence on minerals
questions.solution: **Answer.** NMP 2019 emphasises boosting domestic exploration/production and cutting mineral imports through sustainable mining.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A belt conveyor carries a uniform load of 60 kg per metre of belt at a speed of 2 m/s. Its capacity is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: An ore block of 800 m³ has bulk density 4.5 t/m³. Its tonnage is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.5 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 15 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A main fan develops a pressure of 1800 Pa while passing 45 m³/s of air. The air power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=\dfrac{P\times Q}{1000}$ kW ($P$ in Pa, $Q$ in m³/s).

**Working.** $\dfrac{1800\times45}{1000}=81$ kW.

**Answer.** $81$ kW.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: The Khetri belt in Rajasthan is India's well-known source of:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Khetri copper belt (Jhunjhunu) is worked by Hindustan Copper Limited.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A main fan develops a pressure of 1400 Pa while passing 65 m³/s of air. The air power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=\dfrac{P\times Q}{1000}$ kW ($P$ in Pa, $Q$ in m³/s).

**Working.** $\dfrac{1400\times65}{1000}=91$ kW.

**Answer.** $91$ kW.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Two ore blocks assay: 500 t at 2.5% and 250 t at 1.5%. The combined (tonnage-weighted) grade is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.4 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 40 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.15 m³/min into a roadway carrying 25 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A deposit holds 45 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 3 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{45}{3}=15$ years.

**Answer.** $15$ years.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine pump delivers 0.07 m³/s of water against a head of 130 m at 74% efficiency. The input power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=\dfrac{\rho g Q H}{\eta}$ with $\rho=1000$ kg/m³, $g=9.81$ m/s².

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{1000\times9.81\times0.07\times130}{0.74}\div1000=120.64$ kW.

**Answer.** $120.64$ kW.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A main fan develops a pressure of 2800 Pa while passing 32 m³/s of air. The air power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=\dfrac{P\times Q}{1000}$ kW ($P$ in Pa, $Q$ in m³/s).

**Working.** $\dfrac{2800\times32}{1000}=89.6$ kW.

**Answer.** $89.6$ kW.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: 'Convergence' measured in an underground opening refers to:
options: Closure between the roof and the floor
questions.solution: **Answer.** Convergence is the reduction in roof-to-floor (or rib-to-rib) distance over time.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 1 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 40 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: The gas extracted from coal seams as 'coalbed methane' (CBM) is predominantly:
questions.solution: **Answer.** CBM is mainly methane adsorbed on coal, drained to improve safety and as an energy resource.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A loaded train of mass 11000 kg is hauled up a gradient of 1 in 25. With a tractive (rolling) resistance coefficient of 0.028, the rope pull is approximately _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Under the Mines Act, 1952, no person below the age of ___ years shall be employed in any mine or part thereof:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Section 40 prohibits employment of persons below 18 in a mine.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: An opencast mine removes 550000 m³ of overburden to win 110000 t of ore. The stripping ratio (m³/t) is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A blast detonates 16 kg of explosive per delay and a structure stands 80 m away. The scaled distance $\left(D/\sqrt{W}\right)$ is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scaled distance $SD=\dfrac{D}{\sqrt{W}}$ governs peak particle velocity in blast-vibration control.

**Working.** $\dfrac{80}{\sqrt{16}}=\dfrac{80}{4}=20$.

**Answer.** $20$ m/kg$^{1/2}$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A roadway falls 1 m over a horizontal length of 50 m. Its gradient is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gradient = rise/run expressed as 1 in n.

**Working.** $1$ m over $50$ m $= 1$ in $50$.

**Answer.** 1 in 50.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 15 m² at a mean velocity of 3.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: An ore block of 600 m³ has bulk density 4.2 t/m³. Its tonnage is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 18 m² at a mean velocity of 3 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A dump truck of 60 t payload completes a 18-minute haul cycle over a 450-minute working shift. Its production is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trips $=\dfrac{\text{shift}}{\text{cycle}}$; output $=$ trips $\times$ payload.

**Working.** Trips $=\dfrac{450}{18}=25$; output $=25\times60=1500$ t.

**Answer.** $1500$ t.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A Koepe (friction) winder differs from a drum winder in that it:
options: Drives the rope by friction over a sheave rather than coiling it on a drum
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Koepe system grips the rope by friction on a driving sheave, with a balance/counterweight.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A deposit holds 50 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 5 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{50}{5}=10$ years.

**Answer.** $10$ years.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 2 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 40 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: An opencast blast block has burden 5 m, spacing 5 m, bench height 9 m and rock density 2.6 t/m³. If 112.5 kg of explosive is used, the powder factor (tonnes broken per kg) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** PF $=\dfrac{\text{rock broken (t)}}{\text{explosive (kg)}}$.

**Working.** Volume $=5\times5\times9=225$ m³; mass $=225\times2.6=585$ t; PF $=\dfrac{585}{112.5}=5.2$ t/kg.

**Answer.** $5.2$ t/kg.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A DC mine pump motor draws 2750 W from a 550 V supply. The line current is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: The famous Makrana marble belt lies in which district of Rajasthan?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Makrana, source of the Taj Mahal marble, is in Nagaur district.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: An opencast mine removes 480000 m³ of overburden to win 120000 t of ore. The stripping ratio (m³/t) is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.7 m³/min into a roadway carrying 50 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A roof bolt primarily stabilises strata by:
options: Adding dead weight to the roof
questions.solution: **Answer.** Roof bolts knit weak beds to a stronger bed (suspension/beam-building), improving self-support.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 10 m and height 5 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=2$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times2)=8.55$ MPa.

**Answer.** $8.55$ MPa.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 6 m and height 3 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=2$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times2)=8.55$ MPa.

**Answer.** $8.55$ MPa.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: India's principal rock-phosphate deposit is mined at Jhamarkotra in which Rajasthan district?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Jhamarkotra (Udaipur) is India's largest rock-phosphate source, mined by RSMML.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 1 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 25 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Two ore blocks assay: 150 t at 0.9% and 350 t at 1.8%. The combined (tonnage-weighted) grade is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.4 m³/min into a roadway carrying 15 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A belt conveyor carries a uniform load of 65 kg per metre of belt at a speed of 1.7 m/s. Its capacity is _____.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Transferring surface coordinates and bearing down a vertical shaft to underground workings is called:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Correlation (shaft plumbing) carries surface orientation and position underground.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A fan characteristic is $P=16-0.65Q^2$ and the mine resistance is $P=0.5\,Q^2$. The operating air quantity $Q$ (intersection) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Operating point: fan pressure = mine pressure.

**Working.** $16-0.65Q^2=0.5Q^2\Rightarrow Q^2=\dfrac{16}{1.15}\Rightarrow Q=3.73$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $3.73$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A loaded train of mass 6000 kg is hauled up a gradient of 1 in 15. With a tractive (rolling) resistance coefficient of 0.02, the rope pull is approximately _____.
